source_pr.title: txn: force locking temp index keys in dml in next-gen
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #64241

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Force DMLs to lock temporary index keys. See the issue for details

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) @fzzf678 verified the fix in concurrent DDL-DML scenario
- [ ] No need to test
  > - This is theoretically stricter.

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: A next-gen DDL/DML concurrency scenario was unstable: during `ADD INDEX` backfill (temporary index keys), concurrent pessimistic DML could proceed without locking temp index keys, leading to nondeterministic conflicts/failures under next-gen’s stricter conflict checks.
failure_signature: next-gen add-index + pessimistic txn: intermittent conflict/commit failures caused by missing locks on temporary index keys during backfill
root_cause_explanation: In next-gen, conflict checking applies to more keys (including non-unique index keys). During add-index backfill, temporary index keys can be involved; if DML does not lock those temp index keys, DDL and concurrent pessimistic transactions may observe inconsistent conflict behavior and fail nondeterministically depending on timing. This PR fixes the instability by forcing DML to lock all temporary index keys in next-gen, making the DDL/DML interaction deterministic and preventing spurious conflicts.
fix_pattern: Force DMLs to lock temporary index keys. See the issue for details

Changed files (4): pkg/ddl/tests/indexmerge/BUILD.bazel, pkg/ddl/tests/indexmerge/merge_test.go, pkg/session/test/tidb_test.go, pkg/session/txn.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
